title: Attendance - Ministry of Education
content:
Te taetae ki te kura
Find out when your child should attend school, and how to get support for your child to help them achieve their goals.
- Behaviour management 1
- Legal and compliance 1
- Te taetae ki te kura Attendance information Ākonga | students must attend school every day. Support is available for families of children who are struggling to attend.
Ākonga | students must attend school every day. Support is available for families of children who are struggling to attend.
- Ngā mahere taenga mēnā me whakaiti i ngā hāora me tae atu tō tamaiti ki te kura Attendance plans if your child needs reduced hours at school You can ask to temporarily reduce your child's attendance at school to support their transition into school, or their wellbeing.
You can ask to temporarily reduce your child's attendance at school to support their transition into school, or their wellbeing.
- Me pēhea te whai hāpainga mēnā e rite tonu ana te tamō o tō tamaiti ki te kura How to get support if your child is not attending school regularly There is support available for families of children and young people who are struggling to attend school.
There is support available for families of children and young people who are struggling to attend school.
- Me pēhea te hāpai i ngā taiohi ki te whakatutuki whāinga How we can support young people to achieve their goals Young people need support from their school, teachers, communities, parents and whānau | families to attend school and succeed.
Young people need support from their school, teachers, communities, parents and whānau | families to attend school and succeed.
- Te wehe i te kura i mua i te 16 tau te pakeke Leaving school before 16 You must apply to us for an Early Leaving Exemption if your child wants to leave school before they turn 16.
You must apply to us for an Early Leaving Exemption if your child wants to leave school before they turn 16.
- Tōku pūtake - ko te hāpai i ngā ākonga ki te whakatutuki whāinga My 'why' – supporting students to achieve their goals We’re talking to young people to understand how school and the community are supporting them to achieve their aspirations – their ‘why’. Find out what they had to say in our videos.
We’re talking to young people to understand how school and the community are supporting them to achieve their aspirations – their ‘why’. Find out what they had to say in our videos.
- Ngā pananga, ngā tārewanga, ngā aukatinga me ngā peinga i te kura Stand downs, suspensions, exclusions and expulsions If an ākonga | student is stood down, suspended, excluded, or expelled, they will not be allowed at school.
If an ākonga | student is stood down, suspended, excluded, or expelled, they will not be allowed at school.
- Ngā wā me tae atu tō tamaiti ki te kura When your child should be at school Your child is legally required to be at school every day, unless they are sick. If your child is going to be away, you must let your school know.
Your child is legally required to be at school every day, unless they are sick. If your child is going to be away, you must let your school know.
